name: his-truth-is-marching-on
description: Jon Meacham's portrait of John Lewis as a modern American saint — exploring how Christian faith, nonviolent discipline, and unshakable hope drove a sharecropper's son from cotton fields to the Edmund Pettus Bridge to the halls of Congress.
useCase: Understanding the theological roots of the Civil Rights Movement, Lewis' life as a model of moral leadership, the power of hope in dark times, and the discipline of nonviolence as a way of life.
knownRisks: Explicitly Christian framing (Lewis as saint) may not resonate with secular readers
Meacham focuses on Lewis' moral example rather than critical analysis of his political career
Some events are compressed or simplified for narrative flow
The 'saint' framework has been criticized by some black leaders as reductive
